data.title: What analysts are saying about Coca-Cola's CEO hire
data.description:
Analysts weighed in on Thursday on Coca-Cola Company (KO) announcing that its board of directors has set a succession plan for the beverage giant, with Chief Operating Officer Henrique Braun selected to succeed longtime CEO James Quincey next year.

TD Cowen analyst Robert Moskow said the firm views Braun as a high-quality successor and believes his experience in emerging markets and international operations makes him a strong fit for the CEO position. "When we met with Braun at Coca-Cola's HQ in March, we found his efforts to improve operational performance through increased transparency and more open communications with the company's bottler network as key contributors to the company's recent success,"  Moskow updated on the development.

Bank of America analyst Peter Galbo said the firm was not surprised by Braun’s selection given his increased visibility with investors over the past year. Braun was called a strong choice for Coca-Cola's (KO) bottling system, with his roots as an operator in Latin America positioning him well with the company’s most important partners as the region has experienced some macro challenges and slowdowns in recent quarters.

Galbo and his team highlighted that Braun steps into the CEO role with Coca-Cola (KO) in a much stronger position than several years ago. "KO is delivering faster organic sales growth, operating with great efficiency, and has shifted to a more asset-light model," he added. Importantly, Braun will oversee the finalizing of Coca-Cola's (KO) bottler re-franchising efforts in Africa and India and will serve at a time when KO is still litigating its transfer pricing case with the IRS.

Morgan Stanley analyst Dara Mohsenian was also generally positive about Coca-Cola's (KO) ability to replace Quincey. "Clearly it's a loss for KO, but the company is well prepared in our minds with sustained competitive advantage and is set up for the transition, and we view Braun highly," wrote Mohsenian.

Shares of Coca-Cola (KO) were up 0.8% in early trading on Thursday.

data.title: Most shorted S&P 500 stocks in November
data.description:
Charter Communications (CHTR) emerged as the most shorted S&P 500 (SP500) stock as a percentage of float in November, followed by Moderna (MRNA) and Super Micro Computer (SMCI).

Charter Communications (CHTR) had 19.05% of its shares sold short as of November 28, up from 17.62% at the end of October. The stock carries a Hold rating from Seeking Alpha’s Quant model, with weak scores in growth and momentum, and its one-year share price is down nearly 44%. The company's high debt levels, lack of a dividend, and limited near-term growth catalysts present challenges, with management's focus on future promises not yet delivering immediate results, explained Seeking Alpha analyst Julia Ostian.

Moderna (MRNA) had 19.01% of its shares sold short as of November 28, up from 17.62% at October-end. The biotechnology company's shares have fallen 32% over the past year, largely underperforming the broader market and health care sector fund (XLV), which has advanced 7% in the same period. The company’s recent Q3 report showed a continued decline in COVID vaccine sales. The company's dependence on COVID vaccine sales, amidst dwindling demand and competition, challenges near-term revenue growth and market performance, argued Seeking Alpha analyst Stephen Ayers.

Super Micro Computer (SMCI) took the third spot, with 16.86% of its shares sold short as of November, up from 14.98% in the prior month. Shares have fallen 10% over the past month and 8% over the past year. Super Micro Computer faces ongoing margin pressures, with its gross margin declining to 9.5% and expectations of further contraction, raising concerns about long-term profitability in a competitive AI server market, explained Envision Research.

Here are the top 20 most shorted S&P 500 stocks as a percentage of float, as of November:

  • Charter Communications (CHTR) - Short Interest as a % of float: 19.05%
  • Moderna (MRNA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 19.01%
  • Super Micro Computer (SMCI) - Short Interest as a % of float: 16.86%
  • Lennar (LEN) - Short Interest as a % of float: 16.24%

  • TKO Group Holdings (TKO) - Short Interest as a % of float: 11.08%
  • DaVita Inc. (DVA) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.91%
  • Live Nation Entertainment (LYV) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.75%
  • The Trade Desk (TTD) - Short Interest as a % of float: 10.6%

data.title: Vivakor reaches $65M in debt reduction year-to-date 2025
data.description:
• Vivakor (VIVK) said on Thursday that it has  achieved approximately $65M in total debt reduction year-to-date in 2025, significantly enhancing the company's balance sheet strength and financial flexibility.
  • The year-to-date debt reduction was driven by the completion of Vivakor’s strategic July divestiture and subsequent balance sheet optimization initiatives. 
  • The divestiture of select non-core assets generated approximately $11M in net consideration and eliminated roughly $59M of debt, materially improving the company's credit profile and lowering future interest obligations. 
  • Following this transaction, Vivakor executed targeted restructuring and conversion to equity transactions designed to further enhance liquidity and derisk the capital structure, resulting in an additional $6M of debt reduction and bringing the total to approximately $65M for the year.
  • Vivakor (VIVK) shares were up 2.79% in premarket trading.

data.title: Disney licenses its IP to OpenAI under a $1B equity investment
data.description:
Media conglomerate Disney (DIS) has agreed to make a $1B equity investment in ChatGPT owner OpenAI (OPENAI) and has signed a major licensing agreement under which OpenAI's generative AI video service Sora will have the rights to generate content using Disney's IP.

As part of the three-year agreement, Disney will allow OpenAI to use its portfolio of more than 200 Disney, Marvel, Pixar, and Star Wars characters to generate AI video content. Users will also be able to watch curated selections of Sora-generated videos on Disney+. The agreement does not include any talent likenesses or voices.

Through this agreement, Disney said it will become a major customer of OpenAI, using its APIs to build new products, tools, and experiences, including for Disney+, and deploying ChatGPT for its employees.

Sora and ChatGPT images are expected to start generating fan-inspired videos with Disney’s multi-brand licensed characters in early 2026, the companies said on Thursday.

data.title: Geron to lay off a third of workforce under restructuring program
data.description:
• Geron Corporation (GERN) on Thursday announced plans to lay off nearly a third of its 260 employees as part of a major restructuring initiative under the newly appointed CEO, Harout Semerjian.
  • The Foster City, California-based biotech said that due to the restructuring program, its operating expenses for 2026 are expected to decline compared to the projected operating costs this year.
  • The company added, however, that unspecified restructuring charges, mainly consisting of cash-based costs, will weigh on its bottom line in the near term, and it will provide additional details in a future 8-K filing with the SEC.
  • “We expect this restructuring will have a meaningful impact on our 2026 operating expenses and position Geron to meet the needs of patients,” said Semerjian, a former CEO of cancer drug developer GlycoMimetics who took over GERN’s leadership in August.

data.title: Visa upgraded to Buy at BofA on robust fundamentals, stablecoin potential
data.description:
Bank of America Securities upgraded Visa (V) to Buy from Neutral following the stock's recent underperformance, as analyst Mihir Bhatia sees the payment network company as a premier business.

Visa (V) stock rose 1.3% in Thursday premarket trading. The stock had fallen ~5.1% since Oct. 10, compared with the S&P 500's 5.1% increase.

The stock's current valuation is close to a 10-year trough due to overblown disruption concerns and rotation into AI/risk-on stocks. "We think this presents a compelling opportunity to own one of the best businesses in the world at a reasonable multiple," Bhatia wrote in a note to clients.

The $38B merchant settlement and regulation risks, overall, shouldn't hurt Visa's (V) strong fundamentals, BofA said. Furthermore, stablecoins represent an opportunity for the company, not a threat, and will likely strengthen its position.

"Meanwhile, Visa (V) is one of six companies in the S&P 500 that produces 10%+ revenue growth, double-digit EPS growth, and operating margins >50%, over the past year and only one of two companies that has delivered these metrics annually since 2021," Bhatia said.

BofA's Buy rating on Visa (V) contrasts with the SA Quant rating and the average SA Analyst rating of Hold and aligns with the average Wall Street rating of Buy.

data.title: Dave Kutil replaces Bill Nurthen as the company's new CFO
data.description:
• Research Solutions (NASDAQ: RSSS) announced on Thursday that it has named its controller, Dave Kutil, as interim CFO.
  • The transition followed current CFO and secretary Bill Nurthen, left the company effective December 10, 2025.
  • Kutil has served as controller since joining the company in 2023 and has more than 15 years of financial leadership experience. Before joining the company, he served as controller for the North American subsidiary of Lesaffre and Director of Accounting for Badger Meter.

data.title: Brink's authorized a new share repurchase program worth $750M
data.description:
• Brink's (NYSE:BCO) announced on Thursday the authorization of a new share repurchase program worth $750M.
  • The new $750M authorization was approved by the company's Board on December 10, 2025, and will expire on December 31, 2027.
  • The new authorization is in addition to the previous $500M program, which expires December 31, 2025.
  • Shares +2.33%.

data.title: TSMC may shift to 4-nm production at second Japan plant - Nikkei Asia
data.description:
Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM) (TSMWF) is reportedly evaluating whether to use its second Japanese plant, located in Kumamoto prefecture and scheduled to begin operation in 2027, to make more advanced chips to meet demand for AI-related products.

The world’s largest chipmaker could shift to 4-nanometer production technology for advanced chips used in AI-related applications, Nikkei Asia reported. The shift could lead to design changes and delays for the plant, which began construction in October, according to three sources with knowledge of the matter.  

TSMC originally intended to make 6-nm and 7-nm chips at the second plant. However, since the chipmaker announced plans for the second plant in 2024, demand for 6-nm and 7-nm chips has decreased as leading chipmakers such as Nvidia, Google, Amazon, and Apple have shifted to even more cutting-edge chips.

However, the chipmaker, while announcing the second Japanese facility, stated that its capacity plans would be subject to changes according to customer demand.

TSMC is also considering bringing its advanced chip packaging technology to Japan, which is essential for making chips for AI-computing, two sources told Nikkei. However, they highlighted that none of the plans have been finalized yet.

Nikkei Asia noted that its comparison of photos from the construction site between last month and early December found that construction on the second plant have been paused. Heavy machinery like cranes, excavators and pile drivers has been removed by the early December.

Some suppliers told Nikkei that they were informed that construction would be paused, but the chipmaker did not give any specific reason. The firm also told several suppliers that it does not need additional equipment for Japan for 2026.

TSMC has also previously changed its construction plans according to market demands, such as the redesign of its chip facilities located in Kaohsiung, Southern Taiwan, from 6-nm and 28-nm to cutting edge 2-nm technology.

"TSMC'S projects in Japan are proceeding," the chipmaker told Nikkei Asia, adding that it is currently discussing detailed construction execution plans with its partners.

data.title: Forge UK to become a Registered Auction Agent on LSE’s private securities market
data.description:
• Forge Global (FRGE) said on Thursday that its UK subsidiary, Forge Europe UK, has become a Registered Auction Agent on the London Stock Exchange’s newly launched private securities market.
  • As an RAA, Forge UK will seek to provide eligible investors with streamlined access to buy and sell private company shares through the London Stock Exchange’s auction-based market structure. 
  • Following this announcement, Forge UK customers will gain access to a new channel of regulated European private-market liquidity and enhanced investment opportunities.
